themes: agent-native PM role & system design bar

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Best single-tweet benchmark summary on DeepSeek V4: outperforms Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.4 on SWE-Verified and Codeforces, with open weights — sets the context for how this changes the pricing floor for self-hosters.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's notes on DeepSeek V4 pricing are the most concrete take in the feed: cheapest in both Flash and Pro tiers while staying near frontier quality.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Sharp framing on talent amplification: AI didn't flatten the distribution, it widened it — the new scarce skill is knowing what's worth building, not execution.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Aaron Levie's honest observation that AI doesn't reduce work — it expands scope until you consume all the time you saved, which matters for product strategy around user time budgets.

worthClicking.author: @businessbarista
worthClicking.why: Real-world enterprise AI training report: the biggest aha for senior execs was the shift from AI-as-tool to AI-as-workforce, which is directly relevant to sales enablement framing.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Five-layer agentic architecture diagram pitched as the canonical answer to AI system design interviews — useful reference for product strategy thinking.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concrete claim that the AI PM interview bar has split — old playbook dead at top labs, system design now the deciding round for $1M+ roles.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Anthropic officially admitted Claude Code got dumber — this is the first confirmed post-mortem acknowledgment, closing the loop on a weeks-long thread.

voices.why: Fastest, most accurate model benchmark coverage in the feed with real comparative context — consistently first and correct on DeepSeek V4 and GPT-5.5.
Simon Willison's DeepSeek V4 pricing analysis is the most actionable technical take for anyone making build-vs-buy decisions.
Produces genuinely original product/distribution thinking — the talent amplification post is the best non-benchmark signal in this batch.
Covers AI PM role evolution and agentic architecture with enough specificity to be useful, even if buried in high-volume general content.

examples: @aakashgupta posting 30+ off-topic viral takes (Big Bang Theory salaries, Rihanna, orca brains, pickup truck heights)
@RoundtableSpace all-caps hype clips with no analytical content
newsletter self-promotion posts with zero substance

fading.theme: AI product strategy & utility vs. delight gap
distribution tools for indie devs (Higgsfield Marketing Studio)
